theme.json

This feature has been removed in Outpost v5.0.

Removed in v5.0 — theme.json has been removed. In v5.0, the concept of themes no longer exists. Your site's HTML files live at the site root, and site configuration is handled through the admin panel. There is no theme metadata file to maintain.

What changed

In previous versions, theme.json provided metadata for the admin theme browser (name, version, author, description) and optionally declared Theme Customizer fields. In v5.0:

  • There is no theme browser — your site root is the content source directly.
  • There is no theme switching — there is only one set of templates: the HTML files at your site root.
  • Site configuration (name, branding, etc.) is managed through the admin panel under Settings and Brand.
  • CSS variables and visual customization are managed directly by developers in their CSS files.

Migration

If you are upgrading from a previous version, you can safely delete the theme.json file. No data is lost — the file was metadata-only and did not contain content or configuration that affects rendering.

Related docs

  • Site Structure — how site root files work with Outpost v5.0
  • Setting Up Your Site — structuring HTML files for Outpost
  • Brand Settings — site-wide branding configuration
